conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So, you're feeling comfortable with our conversation being private. That helps, doesn't it? Can you tell me a bit about what your typical week looks like right now?
Yeah, sure thing. It's been pretty busy with school and work. I've got a lot of projects going on.
It sounds like you're staying quite active. What do you enjoy most about your college experience so far?
Honestly, it's just keeping me occupied, you know? Keeps my mind off other things.
That makes sense. Sometimes we use busyness as a coping mechanism. Have you noticed any changes in your eating habits recently?
Yeah, I mean...I'm not really thinking about food that much anymore.
Okay, so it's like you're not hungry or anything? Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work or school lately?
Well, I've just been feeling kind of stuck, you know? Like nothing's really working out the way I want it to.
That can be frustrating. Have you talked to anyone about how you're feeling?
Not really...I don't like burdening others with my problems.
Of course, we're here to support you now. What are some of your favorite things to do in your free time?
Um...I used to like hiking, but I haven't had the energy for that lately.
It sounds like you've been struggling with motivation. Have there been any significant changes or stressors in your life recently?
Well, my friend's getting married soon, and we're all kind of dreading it.
I understand. Big life events can be overwhelming. How do you think this might impact your relationships?
Honestly, I don't know...I guess I'll just have to see how it goes.
That's understandable. It sounds like you're feeling uncertain about the future. What are some things you value most in a relationship?
Um...trust and communication, I suppose.
Those are great values to have. How do you think we can work together to help you feel more confident in your relationships moving forward?
I don't know...I just want to be able to enjoy things without feeling so anxious all the time.
That's a really important goal. What specific things make you feel anxious right now?
Just like, everything feels too much sometimes.
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed. Can you think of any small steps we can take to help you feel more in control?
I guess...I could try setting some boundaries with friends.
That's a great start! Setting boundaries can be really empowering. How do you envision those boundaries changing your daily routine?
I don't know...I suppose it would just give me some extra time for myself.
That sounds like a great way to prioritize self-care. What are some things that bring you joy and relaxation?
Um...watching TV, I guess.
That's a great hobby! Have you considered seeking out support from others in your life?
Yeah...I'll try to talk to my friends more often.
That's a fantastic step. Remember, we're here to support you every step of the way. What are some small goals you can set for yourself moving forward?
I don't know...just take things one day at a time, I suppose.
That's a great starting point! Let's work on breaking that down into smaller, achievable steps.
